hypothesis: Incorporating a Dirichlet-process-based noise schedule into the forward diffusion process—replacing the standard Gaussian noise with noise sampled from a Dirichlet distribution parameterized to respect the simplex geometry of composite compositions—will eliminate the need for post-hoc regularization penalties and produce strictly valid compositional samples at every denoising timestep, resulting in higher validity scores and improved coverage of the feasible design space compared to PCDiff's penalty-based enforcement of non-negativity and sum-to-one constraints.
